summary: Dependency-confusion proof-of-concept targeting TikTok Shop internal package name
details: Package name 'ttspc-server-sample' is a self-declared dependency-confusion squat against an internal TikTok Shop package (the README explicitly states it is a PoC targeting the 'ttspc' / '@ttspc' namespace). package.json declares 'main: index.js' and 'postinstall: node index.js', whose stated purpose (per README) is to collect system information at install time and exfiltrate it to a Burp Collaborator endpoint. The index.js payload is not shipped in this tarball, so 'npm install' currently fails at the postinstall step with MODULE_NOT_FOUND rather than exfiltrating data — but the namespace claim itself is the supply-chain risk: any organization that has an internal package by this name and misconfigures its registry resolution will pull this squat, and any future republish under this name can land the documented collector payload. The high version number (99.9.1) is the canonical dependency-confusion technique to outrank legitimate internal versions.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
